title: Professional LLC (PLLC) Agreement
description: Operating agreement for licensed professionals (doctors, lawyers, CPAs) complying with state professional licensing boards.
whatsCovered: Licensing criteria for all PLLC members
Compliance with state professional licensing board bylaws
Restrictions on transferring ownership to unlicensed individuals
Procedures if a member's state license is suspended or revoked
whenToUse: Use this PLLC operating agreement when forming an LLC for licensed practices where members must hold specific state licenses.

fields.label: Licensed Professional Service
fields.placeholder: e.g. Licensed Medical Practice / Legal Services

clauses.heading: PROFESSIONAL LLC (PLLC) AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This agreement or policy (the "Agreement") is executed as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {party_a_name}, residing/located at {party_a_address} ("First Party"), and {party_b_name}, residing/located at {party_b_address} ("Second Party").

clauses.heading: 1. Core Framework
clauses.body: This agreement establishes the legal rights, bylaws, transfers, waivers, or consent structures for the Professional LLC (PLLC) Agreement. The parties agree to adhere to the terms and declarations set out below.

clauses.heading: 2. State Board Licensing
clauses.body: Members must maintain licenses to practice {professional_service_type} under the authority of {licensing_board}. Loss of license results in member withdrawal.

clauses.heading: General Legal Terms
clauses.body: This document constitutes the entire understanding between the parties. Any amendments must be made in writing and executed by both parties.

clauses.heading: Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Can I customize this legal template?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit this template using the online editor, and add custom schedules or exhibits.

faq.question: Is this agreement binding?
faq.answer: Yes, once signed by all parties, it becomes a legally binding contract under state laws.
